fundamentals.name: MongoDB, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MongoDB, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides general purpose database platform worldwide. The company offers MongoDB Atlas, a hosted multi-cloud database-as-a-service solution; MongoDB Enterprise Advanced, a commercial database server for enterprise customers to run in the cloud, on-premises, or in a hybrid environment; and Community Server, a free-to-download version of its database, which includes the functionality that developers need to get started with MongoDB. It offers professional services comprising consulting and training. The company was formerly known as 10gen, Inc. and changed its name to MongoDB, Inc. in August 2013. MongoDB, Inc. was incorporated in 2007 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue expansion and healthy cash flow, but currently unprofitable on a net basis and carrying significant debt relative to equity.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 12.1%; next earnings date is 2026-05-28.
positives: Revenue growth 26.7% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Free cash flow $463.32M TTM — positive cash generation.
Gross margin 71.7% — healthy core business profitability.
Forward P/E 45.90x — reflects high growth expectations.
Total cash $2.39B vs Total debt $62.83M — strong liquidity position.
negatives: Operating margin 0.0% — breakeven at the operating level.
Return on equity -2.5% and Return on assets -2.4% — negative profitability metrics.
Debt/Equity 2.13 — leverage is high relative to equity.

news.paragraph: MongoDB is set to release its Q1 earnings on May 28th, with strong estimates and a recent surge in stock price following positive analyst actions. Canaccord Genuity, Rosenblatt, and Cantor Fitzgerald all maintained their ratings, with Canaccord and Cantor raising their price targets. This positive sentiment from analysts precedes the earnings report, suggesting confidence in the company's performance.
